summary.promise: Each visual should answer a concrete question, name its boundary, and suggest how to improve the analysis instead of simply asking to be believed.
honesty_contract: Public visuals should stay traceable to row-level or export-level evidence.
If coverage is thin, confidence should fall in plain view.
If a chart is visually strong but analytically weak, the repo should say so directly.
The system should translate what changes when the signal moves, not just that it moved.

dashFieldMap.title: Multi-dimensional bias field
dashFieldMap.purpose: Show whether imbalance is broad across the field or concentrated inside a smaller platform-genre pocket. This is the first orientation surface, not the final verdict.
dashFieldMap.learn: The current synthetic run tracks parity at 0.74 and diversity at 0.71. The bubble field matters because a healthy average can still hide a small cluster where power keeps narrowing.
dashFieldMap.if_changes: If the upper-right cluster collapses when you change year, genre, or media type, the story is fragile. If it survives those slices, the signal is stronger and more portable.
dashFieldMap.improve: Improve this read by increasing sample size, checking the row explorer, and confirming whether the same cluster holds in the network, timeline, and role pattern surfaces.
dashFieldMap.limits: This view is synthetic and descriptive. It can show where to ask harder questions, but it cannot claim a real platform changed behavior without a real catalog lane.
dashFieldMap.scale: For larger companies, treat this as a triage surface: which segment deserves audit time, budget, or human review first.

dashLandscape.title: 3D representation landscape
dashLandscape.purpose: Stress-test the earlier field map in one extra dimension so a point that looked healthy in 2D can be challenged once another axis is visible.
dashLandscape.learn: This view is here to catch summary optimism. If a point keeps its position after a third axis is introduced, the signal is harder to dismiss as a charting artifact.
dashLandscape.if_changes: If the depth layer scrambles the earlier winners, your earlier explanation was under-specified. If the clusters stay coherent, the original story is more structurally sound.
dashLandscape.improve: Pair this with effect sizes and the decision grade. Three dimensions are useful, but they still need quality gates and confidence language.
dashLandscape.limits: 3D is powerful for pattern separation but weaker for exact reading. The value is shape, not precision-by-eye.
dashLandscape.scale: Use it in reviews when you need one image that shows whether a claim survives dimensional pressure before it becomes an executive talking point.

dashNetwork.title: Character interaction topology
dashNetwork.purpose: Show whether representation gaps are also social-topology gaps: who gets clustered together, who gets isolated, and who becomes the center of narrative gravity.
dashNetwork.learn: Current gender homophily is 0.04 with density 0.02. That matters because visibility alone does not tell you whether the same groups keep inheriting the social center.
dashNetwork.if_changes: If homophily weakens once you change genre or platform, the exclusion point may be format-specific. If it stays high, the pattern is more structural.
dashNetwork.improve: Improve this analysis with more row depth, richer interaction definitions, and a comparison against dialogue share so centrality is not mistaken for speech power.
dashNetwork.limits: This is still a modeled graph. It helps reason about topology, but it is not evidence of actual writer-room intent.
dashNetwork.scale: At company scale, this is the surface that translates best into product, content, and policy review because it turns vague fairness claims into relationship structure.

dashTimeline.title: Temporal evolution
dashTimeline.purpose: Separate durable movement from one-year noise. A serious repository should show whether improvement is compounding, stalling, or just moving the imbalance elsewhere.
dashTimeline.learn: The synthetic lane moves from parity 0.67 to 0.76. Read that beside diversity and sentiment, not as a single hero line.
dashTimeline.if_changes: If one metric improves while another stalls, the system is not truly getting better; it is changing where the pressure sits.
dashTimeline.improve: Improve this read with wider time coverage, documented gaps, and publication notes that explain whether the field is measuring releases, recirculation, or catalog resurfacing.
dashTimeline.limits: Trend lines are compelling, but they are only as honest as the date coverage beneath them.
dashTimeline.scale: This is the surface that helps a larger team decide whether to treat a finding as a blip, a trend, or a governance issue that deserves intervention.

dashRoles.title: Role pattern surface
dashRoles.purpose: Reveal whether improvement at the top line is being canceled out by narrow role assignment underneath.
dashRoles.learn: Typecasting matters because a catalog can look broad while still reserving agency, authority, or speech power for the same few archetypes.
dashRoles.if_changes: If the role shape becomes balanced only in one platform or one genre, the system is not broadening; it is localizing the fix.
dashRoles.improve: Improve this analysis by pairing role patterns with dialogue allocation, screen time, and intersectional ratios so role labels do not carry the whole claim alone.
dashRoles.limits: Archetype labels compress nuance. They are useful for pattern detection, not for exhausting the meaning of a character.
dashRoles.scale: This translates well for content and procurement teams because it explains how representation can look better on paper while staying narratively old.

exploreRows.title: Row explorer
exploreRows.purpose: Give contributors and reviewers a direct path back to the evidence so the repo never asks for blind trust.
exploreRows.learn: The current runtime is carrying 5,000 synthetic rows. This view matters because every persuasive summary should still be traceable to a row-level source.
exploreRows.if_changes: If the row slice changes the story, the summary was oversold. That is a feature, not a failure.
exploreRows.improve: Add pagination, stronger schema cues, and more comparison affordances so contributors can pressure the data faster.
exploreRows.limits: Raw rows are necessary, but they do not explain themselves. They need contracts, quality checks, and honest labeling.
exploreRows.scale: For larger teams this is where debugging, procurement review, and contributor onboarding start.

learnDataSystem.title: Data engineering operating model
learnDataSystem.purpose: Explain how the pipeline behaves before anyone is asked to trust the visuals it produces.
learnDataSystem.learn: There are 2 public contracts and 4 explicit quality highlights in the current system surface. That is the spine underneath the charts.
learnDataSystem.if_changes: If quality gates fail or contracts drift, the visual layer should become less confident immediately.
learnDataSystem.improve: Keep expanding lineage, refresh posture, and coverage notes so a contributor can see where a claim is strong, thin, or intentionally withheld.
learnDataSystem.limits: Documentation can still drift if it is not regenerated from the same backend that serves the UI.
learnDataSystem.scale: This is the part that makes the repo feel operational rather than theatrical to senior engineers and data leads.

scorecardVerdict.title: Decision grade
scorecardVerdict.purpose: Compress complexity into a usable decision signal only after the nuance has already been made visible.
scorecardVerdict.learn: The current screen-time gini proxy is 0.24. The grade matters only because the repo lets a reader walk backward into the ingredients.
scorecardVerdict.if_changes: If a small shift flips the grade dramatically, the compression is too brittle and needs more context.
scorecardVerdict.improve: Keep effect sizes, trend fit, and caveats close to the grade so no one mistakes compression for full truth.
scorecardVerdict.limits: A grade is useful for decisions, not for understanding the full field. It should route someone into the evidence, not replace it.
scorecardVerdict.scale: This is the executive surface: fast to read, but only credible if the lower layers remain inspectable.

exploreMedia.title: Media-format comparison
exploreMedia.purpose: Separate platform effects from format effects so a pattern is not mistaken for a platform truth when it is actually being driven by animation, film, series, or reality structure.
exploreMedia.learn: This view exists to answer a practical question: does the pattern still hold once the media format changes, or was the original signal just hitching a ride on one production format?
exploreMedia.if_changes: If the signal disappears when media type changes, the earlier claim needs to narrow. If it stays stable, the pattern is more portable.
exploreMedia.improve: Improve this lane with clearer cohort sizes, stronger notes about edge cases, and side-by-side confidence language for thin slices.
exploreMedia.limits: Format labels are useful but incomplete. They should guide follow-up analysis, not stand in for it.
exploreMedia.scale: For larger teams, this helps explain whether a problem belongs to the whole system or just one format lane.

exploreGenre.title: Genre-specific pressure
exploreGenre.purpose: Show where a broad claim starts to splinter once genre enters, because many fairness or virality stories turn out to be genre-specific rather than universal.
exploreGenre.learn: The value here is not only the averages. It is seeing whether one genre keeps absorbing the same advantage while other lanes behave very differently.
exploreGenre.if_changes: If a result flips direction across genres, the repo should stop speaking in global terms and get more exact.
exploreGenre.improve: Improve this with better genre coverage notes, more year-aware comparisons, and clearer handling of miscellaneous or unclassified rows.
exploreGenre.limits: Genre labels are noisy and culturally loaded. They are a useful organizing surface, not a perfect ontology.
exploreGenre.scale: This becomes a budgeting and programming surface when teams need to know which lanes are actually driving the issue.

insightsSignals.title: Generated findings layer
insightsSignals.purpose: Translate the analytical field into readable conclusions without severing those conclusions from their evidence.
insightsSignals.learn: These generated findings matter only when they stay traceable to filters, cohorts, and metrics. Their job is to shorten time-to-understanding, not replace inspection.
insightsSignals.if_changes: If the generated wording changes dramatically under a small slice adjustment, the finding is unstable and should be presented more cautiously.
insightsSignals.improve: Keep attaching confidence, sample shape, and contradiction checks so the writing stays honest instead of polished.
insightsSignals.limits: Plain-language summaries are convenient, but they can over-smooth complexity if they are not constantly tied back to structure.
insightsSignals.scale: For larger companies, this is the handoff layer between deep analysis and people who need to act on it quickly.

libraryPropagationStudio.title: Bias propagation studio
libraryPropagationStudio.purpose: Show how a pattern becomes a system: where a small skew enters, how it compounds, and why downstream surfaces can end up looking natural even when the starting point was not.
libraryPropagationStudio.learn: This is the explanatory spine for people who can sense that something feels off but need to see the chain from input to consequence.
libraryPropagationStudio.if_changes: If one propagation step dominates the whole story, that is the intervention point. If many small steps compound, the system needs layered correction rather than one patch.
libraryPropagationStudio.improve: Improve it by adding more counterfactual examples and clearer notes about where the repo is modeling versus directly observing.
libraryPropagationStudio.limits: Propagation diagrams can look more certain than they are. They should declare where the evidence is direct and where it is inferential.
libraryPropagationStudio.scale: This is useful for ethics, policy, and engineering audiences because it connects abstract harm to a tractable chain.

mvPowerLaw.title: Power-law attention tail
mvPowerLaw.purpose: Test whether attention is being distributed broadly or whether a tiny winner tail is doing most of the work.
mvPowerLaw.learn: The real music lane covers 100 songs and 10,591,031,907 views. The power-law fit matters because virality claims usually sound organic long after the field has already become tail-dominated.
mvPowerLaw.if_changes: If the tail gets steeper, breakout concentration is easing. If it gets heavier, scale and incumbent reach are doing more of the outcome shaping.
mvPowerLaw.improve: Improve this with wider cohorts, better year segmentation, and side-by-side cohort comparisons so one leaderboard does not stand in for a market.
mvPowerLaw.limits: A heavy tail is descriptive, not accusatory. It tells you how concentrated attention looks, not why any individual song won.
mvPowerLaw.scale: This is where larger companies can see whether discovery infrastructure is broadening the field or mainly amplifying incumbent advantage.

mvInequality.title: Inequality of attention
mvInequality.purpose: Turn the winner-take-most feeling into measurable concentration so the conversation can move beyond impression.
mvInequality.learn: The current real-data Gini is 0.70 and top-10 share is 72%. That is the concrete shape of the field.
mvInequality.if_changes: If concentration drops while views remain strong, the market may be opening. If concentration rises, the field is becoming easier to describe but harder to enter.
mvInequality.improve: Keep cohort segmentation visible so inequality is not mistaken for one universal market law.
mvInequality.limits: Concentration metrics are blunt. They tell you where the center is, not what artistic or platform forces created it.
mvInequality.scale: Useful for strategy, procurement, and creator economics because it shows whether success is being distributed or hoarded.

mvCorrelation.title: Correlation architecture
mvCorrelation.purpose: Separate what travels with scale from what appears to matter on its own.
mvCorrelation.learn: Raw correlation is only the first pass. The partial layer matters because it tests whether a feature still holds once channel size stops carrying it.
mvCorrelation.if_changes: If a feature looks strong in the raw view but collapses in the partial view, scale was doing most of the talking.
mvCorrelation.improve: Improve this lane with more contrastive explanations, cleaner feature provenance, and clearer labeling of pre- versus post-publication fields.
mvCorrelation.limits: Correlation can suggest where to look harder, but it does not prove mechanism.
mvCorrelation.scale: This is the layer that keeps a music or media strategy conversation from drifting into superstition.

mvArchetypes.title: Viral archetypes
mvArchetypes.purpose: Turn a noisy leaderboard into a handful of reusable release shapes so readers can reason about strategies, not just songs.
mvArchetypes.learn: The clusters matter because they tell you whether success keeps arriving through the same small set of release patterns.
mvArchetypes.if_changes: If archetypes collapse into one blob when the feature mix changes, the segmentation is decorative. If they remain separable, it is carrying real structure.
mvArchetypes.improve: Keep naming the cluster assumptions, exposing cluster counts, and checking whether the groupings survive under different windows.
mvArchetypes.limits: Cluster names are interpretive. They help memory and discussion, but they should not be mistaken for immutable market categories.
mvArchetypes.scale: This is where product, label, and platform teams can start comparing release tactics without flattening everything into one average.

mvNetwork.title: Tag universe
mvNetwork.purpose: Map the neighboring language around music releases so the repo can show whether discovery is broadening or just recirculating a familiar cluster.
mvNetwork.learn: This surface is useful because it shows bridge tags, central tags, and isolated tag pockets that are easy to miss in a table.
mvNetwork.if_changes: If the same tags remain central under different cohorts, the field is more repetitive than its long tail suggests.
mvNetwork.improve: Improve it by keeping tag cleaning visible, adding time windows, and showing when a dense cluster is being carried by only a few channels.
mvNetwork.limits: Tags are packaging signals as much as musical signals, so they should be read with that commercial bias in mind.
mvNetwork.scale: For search, discovery, and editorial teams, this becomes a practical map of how the catalog is being framed.

mvPredictability.title: Predictability surface
mvPredictability.purpose: Force the repo to be honest about how much can actually be forecast before publication.
mvPredictability.learn: The current out-of-fold ensemble R² is 0.67. That matters because a low but honest score is more useful than a glamorous model with leakage.
mvPredictability.if_changes: If predictive lift rises with clean features, the field has legible structure. If it only rises when you leak post-publication data, the model is pretending.
mvPredictability.improve: Keep leakage blocks explicit, add calibration notes, and broaden the feature audit so contributors can improve the model without gaming the story.
mvPredictability.limits: Prediction is not explanation. Even a strong model does not tell you whether the mechanism is fair or healthy.
mvPredictability.scale: For larger teams this is the bridge between analytical curiosity and something operationally useful.

mvResonance.title: Resonance mapper
mvResonance.purpose: Give the repo a way to talk about repeated attention pressure without pretending it can observe hidden platform internals.
mvResonance.learn: This visual is useful because it makes the public shape of repetition legible: duration fit, release pressure, concentration, and tag/title echo can still reveal an environment optimized for recurrence.
mvResonance.if_changes: If resonance narrows and stabilizes around the same tracks, the field is leaning toward manufactured repeatability. If it diffuses, discovery may be opening up.
mvResonance.improve: Improve it with better cohort windows, more public context data, and clearer translation between repeat pressure and human consequence.
mvResonance.limits: This is a public proxy, not a claim about hidden recommender code or audio waveforms.
mvResonance.scale: This is where virality stops sounding mystical and starts looking environmental.

mvWhatIf.title: What-if predictor
mvWhatIf.purpose: Give readers a safe way to stress the public model and see how one feature change alters the rank story.
mvWhatIf.learn: This matters because most people need to feel the slope of the model before they understand what the summary metrics are saying.
mvWhatIf.if_changes: If a tiny control change produces a wild output swing, the model or feature space needs better calibration.
mvWhatIf.improve: Improve it with clearer uncertainty language, cohort-aware presets, and more visible notes about what the model does not know.
mvWhatIf.limits: A simulator is a directional instrument, not a guarantee of outcome.
mvWhatIf.scale: This is the most approachable bridge from research to practical planning because it lets non-specialists test consequences directly.

mvBias.title: Bias in the top 100
mvBias.purpose: Show that virality is not only a music question. It is also a distribution, exposure, and advantage question.
mvBias.learn: Publication year coverage is 19% and notation link rate is 0%. That honesty matters because missing context should lower confidence, not be paved over.
mvBias.if_changes: If gender, genre, or language concentration shifts under a new role lens, the conclusion should change in consequence, not in facts.
mvBias.improve: Keep growing country, year, and notation coverage so this section can move from structural hints to deeper musical and market evidence.
mvBias.limits: This lane uses public data and public inference. It should stay explicit about what is observed, what is classified, and what is still unknown.
mvBias.scale: For entertainment, platform, and creator teams, this is the section that translates abstract bias into market shape.

mv3DLandscape.title: 3D virality landscape
mv3DLandscape.purpose: Give the reader one higher-dimensional pass on views, virality, and channel scale at the same time so strategy does not get trapped in a flat ranking.
mv3DLandscape.learn: The point is not the spectacle. The point is to see whether breakout songs occupy genuinely different terrain or are just scaled-up versions of the same release pattern.
mv3DLandscape.if_changes: If the 3D shape shows clear separation, strategy families are more believable. If it turns into a compressed cloud, the market is behaving more uniformly than the labels suggest.
mv3DLandscape.improve: Improve it with stronger cohort filters and better explanatory labels so the depth dimension stays legible to non-specialists.
mv3DLandscape.limits: 3D scenes are intuitive for shape and weaker for precise reading. They need companion annotations.
mv3DLandscape.scale: This is useful in demos and reviews because it gives one quick sense of whether the field has multiple lanes or one narrow groove.

mvSongsExplorer.title: Public songs explorer
mvSongsExplorer.purpose: Keep the real-data lane falsifiable by letting readers inspect, sort, and challenge the rows beneath the story.
mvSongsExplorer.learn: The current score-aware lane has 0 discovered local music files and 0 matched catalog songs. That tells the reader how deep the note-aware bridge actually goes today.
mvSongsExplorer.if_changes: If the summary feels persuasive but the rows or note links look thin, the repo should say so plainly.
mvSongsExplorer.improve: Keep adding pagination, stronger schema cues, and more score-linked public files so contributors can build on a real substrate instead of a screenshot.
mvSongsExplorer.limits: An explorer is only useful if the repository keeps the row layer honest and updated.
mvSongsExplorer.scale: This is the collaborator surface: the place where people can verify, extend, and actually contribute.
